chunk_summary: Safe macronutrient sequencing in facility-based management of severe acute malnutrition involves stabilization with controlled feeding (F-75), transitioning to rehabilitation (F-100), and ensuring clinical recovery before discharge, following national and WHO guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the stabilization-to-rehabilitation sequence in severe acute malnutrition management.
Learn the feeding protocols for F-75 and F-100 during different phases of treatment.
Recognize discharge criteria and follow-up protocols for children recovering from severe acute malnutrition.
key_facts: Admission criteria include weight-for-length/height < -3 SD, MUAC <115 mm (6–59 months), or bilateral pitting edema.
Initial feeding uses F-75 for controlled energy and protein delivery during stabilization.
Transition to F-100 or catch-up diet occurs when appetite returns and edema resolves.
Discharge requires at least 15% weight gain from admission weight, edema resolution, and reliable feeding.
Fluid management prioritizes oral/nasogastric rehydration; IV fluids are avoided unless shock is present.
reference_quote: The WHO 2023 wasting guideline updates global recommendations; in India, follow national FBMC and nutrition rehabilitation centre protocols while tracking evolving guidance.
document_key_topics: Severe acute malnutrition stabilization and rehabilitation
Feeding protocols for F-75 and F-100
